title: coleus foetidus
intro: Coleus foetidus, also known as the stinkweed or bitter coleus, is a perennial plant species native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its bitter and slightly sweet taste, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve flatulence. The leaves and roots of the plant are used to prepare herbal remedies, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, treating fever, and aiding in the relief of respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to make a tea, and it can also be used to make a tincture or dried powder for later use.
